message:
fix(sync): prevent lost notify_one baton when broadcast supersedes original waiter set

The Notify baton-passing protocol had a subtle interaction between
notify_one and notify_waiters: when a waiter was woken by notify_one
but a subsequent broadcast updated all waiter generations, dropping
the original waiter would pass the baton via pass_baton() which
stores a notification if no active waiter exists. This stored
notification could then be consumed by an unrelated later waiter,
violating the intended single-delivery semantic.

New behavior:
- pass_baton_if_waiter_exists() passes the baton only to a currently
  active waiter and does NOT store a replacement notification when
  none exists — used when the generation has advanced (broadcast
  already covered the original set)
- Notified::poll: when a generation-changed waiter that was also
  notified by notify_one is resolved, it calls
  pass_baton_if_waiter_exists to hand off only to post-broadcast
  waiters
- Notified::drop: distinguishes generation-advanced vs same-generation
  cancellation to choose the appropriate baton-passing strategy

tests/notify_lost_baton.rs:
- Add module doc comment
- Fix missing trailing newline

Minor: import reordering (rustfmt canonical), remove extra blank lines

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
